goal: Port structural.redundancy (sibling_calls) to Structure; rename to canonical

summary.title: Port structural.redundancy to Structure; rename to canonical
summary.what: Adds Language vocabulary (call_node_types, extract_callee_name, trivial_callees) with overrides for Python/C/C++/Ruby. Adds Structure.callees_of() and Structure.redundant_siblings() view methods plus RedundancyPair frozen record. Renames rule file sibling_calls.py → redundancy.py, function run_sibling_call_redundancy → run_redundancy, test file test_sibling_calls.py → test_redundancy.py. Drops legacy_v2_shim. Deletes slop/_structural/sibling_calls.py (729 lines). Full suite 533/533.
summary.why: Fifth structural shim retirement plus the file/function rename to match the registry's canonical name (structural.redundancy, already in place since the v2.0 substrate cutover). The legacy kernel duplicated per-language call-extraction logic (_gather_callees / _gather_callees_c / _gather_callees_cpp) that now collapses into a single substrate walk via tabular dispatch on the new Language vocabulary.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.redundancy (sibling_calls) to Structure; rename to canonical

decisions.chose: Split callee extraction into three Language hooks instead of one consolidated method
decisions.rationale: call_node_types() lets the helper drive the DFS uniformly; extract_callee_name(node, content) is the per-grammar variant; trivial_callees() is the filter list. This keeps the per-grammar code small (just the parts that actually differ) and the helper's DFS walks once regardless of language. A consolidated extract_callees(body, content) -> set[str] would have forced the DFS to live in every grammar.

decisions.chose: Universal length / dunder filters in the helper, not the Language
decisions.rationale: Length < 3 and dunder names are universal noise filters. Pushing them into Language.trivial_callees() would require every grammar to re-declare them. The helper applies them once per call.

decisions.chose: Match canonical name with file + function rename, not just registry alias
decisions.rationale: The registry has used 'structural.redundancy' as the canonical name since the v2.0 cutover, but the rule file and function still carried the legacy 'sibling_calls' / 'sibling_call_redundancy' prefix. Renaming brings the implementation surface in line with the user-facing name. Per the naming spec, the rule is named for the smell it detects (redundancy), not the substrate technique that detects it (sibling-call analysis).

decisions.chose: Use Callable.parent dotted-detection to skip nested functions
decisions.rationale: The legacy kernel iterated tree.root_node.children to find top-level functions only. The substrate already records the parent qualname; nested functions have a dotted parent ('module.outer' rather than 'module'). Single check replaces the per-grammar 'walk root_node.children only' pattern.

review_notes: Rule registry name 'structural.redundancy' is unchanged — already the canonical name. No _compat.py change needed because users targeting the old technical name 'sibling_calls' would have been targeting an internal function name, not a rule name.
Per-language tests (test_c, test_cpp, test_ruby) migrated to pass Structure via inline Tree.scan(); 1-line change each.

The clones false-positive observation from the previous intent (tabular-dispatch classmethods flagging as clones) does NOT apply here — the redundancy rule has both a min_shared AND min_score requirement, and tabular-dispatch methods typically share only the helper they dispatch to (one shared call), which falls below min_shared=3.

architectural_claims: Per-grammar call-extraction logic is now declared by tabular dispatch via Language.call_node_types + Language.extract_callee_name + Language.trivial_callees, eliminating the per-grammar DFS duplication in the legacy kernel.
Top-level-only function filtering uses Callable.parent's dotted-qualname check, removing the need for grammar-specific 'iterate root_node.children only' patterns.
behavioral_changes: run_redundancy signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc); function renamed from run_sibling_call_redundancy.
Rule file path changes from slop/structure/rules/sibling_calls.py to slop/structure/rules/redundancy.py.
Test file path changes from tests/test_rules/test_sibling_calls.py to tests/test_rules/test_redundancy.py.
Summary fields 'functions_analyzed' and 'files_searched' are no longer reported (substrate exposes those at a different layer if needed).
migration_notes: Custom code importing run_sibling_call_redundancy from slop.structure.rules.sibling_calls will break. The replacement is slop.structure.rules.redundancy.run_redundancy.
Test code importing sibling_call_redundancy_kernel will break (legacy kernel deleted). Use Tree + Structure.redundant_siblings() instead.
